title: TSMC expects 'strong, multi-year' demand for AI chips as it ramps up Arizona investment
author: Daniel Nenni
text:
Daniel Nenni
Founder
Staff member

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company expects demand for artificial intelligence chips to remain strong for several years, reinforcing its decision to expand its manufacturing footprint in Arizona. Chief Financial Officer Wendell Huang said TSMC is pleased with the progress of its first Arizona plant, which is operating with production yields comparable to the company’s leading facilities in Taiwan.

TSMC plans to invest an additional $100 billion in Arizona, raising its total planned investment in the state to $265 billion. Its expanding campus is expected to include 12 fabrication and advanced-packaging facilities, along with a research and development center. A second factory is preparing for equipment installation, construction of a third is underway, and early work has begun on a fourth plant and an advanced-packaging facility.

However, TSMC faces obstacles, including shortages of construction workers and infrastructure constraints. Huang said the company will cooperate with the U.S. government to address these issues.

Despite its American expansion, TSMC will continue investing heavily in Taiwan, where it is building 13 advanced facilities. The company believes its newest technologies must initially be developed close to its Taiwanese research and operations teams before production can be transferred overseas. Geopolitical tensions and export-control concerns remain important risks.
